topic: Agriculture: Cash Crops, Plantation Crops & Oilseeds

questions.text: Which state is the largest producer of cotton in India?
questions.explanation: Gujarat is India's largest cotton producer, not Maharashtra. Maharashtra is second (Vidarbha region). Gujarat also grows the most groundnut and castor — it dominates the oilseed and cash crop belt of western India.
questions.source: SSC CGL / SSC CHSL previous years

questions.text: Which state is the largest producer of jute in India?
questions.explanation: West Bengal produces about 80% of India's jute. The Hugli River belt in West Bengal has dozens of jute mills. Jute is called the 'Golden Fibre' and grows in the hot, humid Ganga-Brahmaputra delta alluvial soil.

questions.text: Which state is the largest producer of tea in India?
questions.explanation: Assam is India's largest tea-producing state by volume, producing strong malty CTC tea from the Brahmaputra valley. West Bengal (Darjeeling) is most famous for quality but produces far less. Guwahati in Assam is also the world's largest tea auction centre.

questions.text: Who is known as the 'Father of the White Revolution' in India?
questions.explanation: Verghese Kurien is the Father of the White Revolution (Operation Flood, 1970–1996). He built Amul (Anand Milk Union Limited) and the National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) at Anand, Gujarat, making India the world's largest milk producer. MS Swaminathan is Father of the Green Revolution.

questions.text: Which state produces the most sugarcane in India, and which state has the highest sugarcane yield per hectare?
options: UP for both production and yield
questions.explanation: This is a classic production vs. productivity trap. Uttar Pradesh produces the most sugarcane in absolute quantity (Meerut-Muzaffarnagar belt). Tamil Nadu (and Karnataka) achieve the highest sucrose content and yield per hectare because of their tropical conditions. SSC exploits this distinction repeatedly.

questions.text: Darjeeling tea was the first Indian product to receive a Geographical Indication (GI) tag. In what year?
questions.explanation: Darjeeling tea received India's first Geographical Indication (GI) tag in 2004 under the Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999. A GI tag certifies that only tea grown in Darjeeling hills can be marketed as 'Darjeeling Tea'.

questions.text: India is the world's largest producer of which industrial oil?
questions.explanation: India is the world's largest producer of castor oil. Gujarat is the dominant state for castor. Castor oil is primarily used industrially — lubricants, biodiesel, polymers, and cosmetics — NOT as a cooking oil. This tricky detail (industrial vs edible) is what SSC tests.

questions.text: Which of the following commodity boards is headquartered in Kolkata?
questions.explanation: Tea Board of India is headquartered in Kolkata (Calcutta), reflecting the historical Bengal-Assam tea trade. Coffee Board = Bengaluru; Rubber Board = Kottayam, Kerala; Spices Board = Kochi, Kerala; Coir Board = Kochi, Kerala; Tobacco Board = Guntur, AP.

questions.text: Which river belt is the centre of India's jute manufacturing industry?
questions.explanation: The Hugli River in West Bengal is the heart of India's jute mill industry. Towns like Rishra, Serampore, Barrackpore, and Uluberia all have jute mills along a 100-km Hugli riverfront stretch. Raw jute from Bengal and Bihar fields comes downstream and is processed here.

questions.text: India is referred to as an 'oil-deficit' country despite being a large oilseed producer. Which edible oil does India import the most?
questions.explanation: India imports the most palm oil (from Malaysia and Indonesia), which accounts for about 60–65% of India's total edible oil imports. Palm oil is cheapest globally. India also imports sunflower oil (from Ukraine/Russia). Groundnut oil is domestically produced and not imported.

questions.text: Which district in Andhra Pradesh is the world's largest centre for Flue-Cured Virginia (FCV) tobacco trade?
questions.explanation: Guntur (Andhra Pradesh) is Asia's — and effectively the world's — largest FCV tobacco auction and trading centre. FCV tobacco from Guntur and Krishna districts is premium grade exported to international cigarette manufacturers. Guntur is also India's largest chilli market.

questions.text: The 'Coir Capital of the World' is which city in India?
questions.explanation: Alappuzha (Alleppey) in Kerala is called the 'Coir Capital of the World.' Coir is the fibre extracted from coconut husks and is used for mats, ropes, and geotextiles. The Coir Board of India is headquartered in Kochi, but the production and trading hub is Alappuzha.

questions.text: Which state is the largest producer of coffee in India?
questions.explanation: Karnataka produces about 70% of India's coffee, making it the undisputed largest coffee producer. Coorg (Kodagu) and Chikmagalur are the famous coffee districts. Bababudan Hills in Chikmagalur is where coffee was first cultivated in India in the 17th century. Kerala is 2nd (~20%), Tamil Nadu 3rd (~10%).
questions.source: SSC CGL / SSC CHSL — very frequently asked

questions.text: What is the largest producing state for natural rubber in India?
questions.explanation: Kerala produces about 91% of India's natural rubber. The Rubber Board of India is headquartered in Kottayam, Kerala — known as the 'Rubber City of India.' Tripura is a fast-growing rubber producer in the northeast. India's rubber goes almost entirely to the domestic tyre industry.

questions.text: In which of the following agricultural revolutions is Verghese Kurien associated?
questions.explanation: Verghese Kurien led the White Revolution (Operation Flood, 1970–1996), which made India the world's largest milk producer. He built Amul and NDDB at Anand, Gujarat. MS Swaminathan = Green Revolution; Yellow Revolution = Oilseeds (Technology Mission on Oilseeds, 1986); Blue Revolution = Fisheries.
questions.source: SSC CGL / Railway / Bank PO — very frequent

questions.text: Black Pepper is known as the 'King of Spices.' Which state is its largest producer in India?
questions.explanation: Kerala is the largest black pepper producer in India — particularly Wayanad, Idukki, and Kannur districts. Black pepper is called 'King of Spices' and 'Black Gold' — it was the primary reason European powers sought sea routes to India. Cardamom (Queen of Spices) also comes from Kerala (Idukki's Cardamom Hills).
questions.source: SSC CGL / SSC CHSL — frequently asked
